title: Oxystophyllum oligadenium
taxonomy: Oxystophyllum oligadenium is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, comprising over 30,000 species worldwide. Oxystophyllum oligadenium is a species of orchid, and it is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is one of the largest and most widespread groups within the orchid family. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Oxystophyllum oligadenium and other orchids.
uses: Oxystophyllum oligadenium has been used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia, specifically in Malaysia and Thailand. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
